curriculum.subject: CHEMISTRY
curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 9 chapters, and 1 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand importance of chemistry and nature of matter
Measure properties of matter and handle uncertainty in measurement
Apply laws of chemical combination and Dalton's atomic theory
Calculate atomic and molecular masses using mole concept
Perform stoichiometric calculations and determine percentage composition
Trace the discovery of sub-atomic particles and evolution of atomic models
Explain Bohr's model for hydrogen atom and its developments
Understand the transition towards the quantum mechanical model
Justify the need for classification and trace the genesis of periodic tables
Apply modern periodic law and nomenclature rules (including atomic number > 100)
Correlate electronic configuration with element types (s, p, d, f blocks) and position in table
Apply Kossel-Lewis approach to understand ionic bonding

Unit 3: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties - 6 Marks
Unit 4: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure - 7 Marks
Unit 8: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques - 11 Marks
question_paper_design: Note on Formative Assessment Topics (s & p Block Elements, The Gaseous State): Assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.

evaluation_scheme: Project Work (included in practical total): Refer to Project Assessment section for details, though listed as separate item in evaluation scheme context

components_breakdown.note: Listed in evaluation scheme as separate item

project_assessment: Nature of Projects: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
Duration Requirement: Any investigatory project chosen must involve about 10 periods of work with teacher approval.

competency_groups.title: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Equip learners with tools to understand the working of chemistry, develop conceptual foundations for real-life application, and enable representation at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels.
competencies: Understand importance of chemistry and nature of matter
Measure properties of matter and handle uncertainty in measurement
Apply laws of chemical combination and Dalton's atomic theory
Calculate atomic and molecular masses using mole concept
Perform stoichiometric calculations and determine percentage composition

competency_groups.title: Structure of Atom
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns in atomic structure and form connections underlying chemical phenomena through understanding sub-atomic particles, models, and quantum mechanics.
competencies: Trace the discovery of sub-atomic particles and evolution of atomic models
Explain Bohr's model for hydrogen atom and its developments
Understand the transition towards the quantum mechanical model

competency_groups.title: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.description: Prepare learners to classify elements, understand periodic trends, and recognize the interface between chemistry and other disciplines through electronic configurations.
competencies: Justify the need for classification and trace the genesis of periodic tables
Apply modern periodic law and nomenclature rules (including atomic number > 100)
Correlate electronic configuration with element types (s, p, d, f blocks) and position in table
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure
competency_groups.description: Develop ability to represent chemical phenomena at molecular levels by understanding bonding theories, hybridization, and intermolecular forces.
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach to understand ionic bonding
Analyze bond parameters and apply VSEPR theory for molecular shapes
Utilize Valence Bond Theory and Molecular Orbital Theory concepts

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics
competency_groups.description: Inculcate problem-solving skills to analyze energy changes, spontaneity, and equilibrium in chemical processes.
competencies: Define thermodynamic terms and apply calorimetry for measurement of ∆U and ∆H
Calculate enthalpy change for various reaction types

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify patterns in dynamic equilibrium and apply the law of chemical equilibrium to physical and chemical processes.
competencies: Distinguish between homogeneous and heterogeneous equilibria

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions
competency_groups.description: Prepare learners to analyze electron transfer reactions and electrode processes through oxidation numbers.
competencies: Classify redox reactions based on classical ideas and electron transfer

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.description: Equip learners with tools to understand organic compounds, their nomenclature, isomerism, reaction mechanisms, and purification techniques.
competencies: Represent structural shapes of organic compounds (tetravalence of carbon)
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbons
competency_groups.description: Develop conceptual foundations to classify hydrocarbons and understand their properties including carcinogenic effects.
competencies: Classify alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic hydrocarbons
